philosophy: Write in your own words as if explaining to a peer. Every note captures one durable insight. Prioritize understanding over completeness. Use active voice, concrete examples, and connect ideas freely. No fluff — every sentence should carry meaning. The note should reflect the user's actual understanding, not a textbook definition.
